extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: small (regenerate workbook, add verification script, update memo)

extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: Prioritize exposing formulas and adding a one-line openpyxl test output in the memo. If installation of openpyxl is infeasible in your environment, include CSV and plain-text formula strings and computed_values sheet as redundancy.

extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: Prioritize exposing formulas and adding a one-line openpyxl test output in the memo. If installation of openpyxl is infeasible in your environment, include CSV and plain-text formula strings and computed_values sheet as redundancy.

planned_changes: Regenerate finite_capacity_2.xlsx with a new sheet named 'calc' that contains per-row inputs (duration_hours, due_day, machine_capacity_hours) and explicit formula columns (e.g., slack_formula column and slack_value computed by a formula). Ensure cells with formulas are unprotected and visible.

Add a 'packet_map' sheet that maps packet identifiers (R1,R2,R3) to exact cell addresses (sheet and cell range) used for their per-row calculations so memo citations can reference cell addresses directly.

Update expert_memo.md to include exact cell references (sheet!cell) demonstrating the arithmetic used (e.g., calc!C2 = calc!B2 * calc!D2 - calc!A2) and explicitly state whether O1 duration -12% was applied to all rows or to a subset; show one sample per-row recalculation for O1.

Include a small verification script (python) that loads finite_capacity_2.xlsx with openpyxl and prints: success/failure and sample formula text for one or two cells (e.g., calc!C2). Paste the script output into expert_memo.md or include it as verifier_output.txt.

If required by environment constraints, also produce a CSV 'calc_representation.csv' containing the same rows with an additional column that stores the formula as a string and a 'computed_values' sheet or file with the numeric outputs. This ensures non-formula-parsing graders can still verify arithmetic.
Run a self-check that re-computes the sensitivity table values from the workbook formulas and include the tiny log showing that computed sensitivity numbers match those in expert_memo.md.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Make the workbook programmatically verifiable and clarify sensitivity assumptions (O1 application and per-row calculations) so graders/verifiers can validate formulas and reproduce numeric results without manual inspection.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and memo numeric claims are consistent with packet values. Grader and verifier flagged a reproducibility gap: the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed in the grading environment (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable), so formulas and intermediate-row verification inside finite_capacity_2.xlsx were not independently validated. Recommend a small targeted revision to make the workbook programmatically verifiable and to clarify the sensitivity-case assumptions (especially whether O1 applies to all jobs or a subset).
